---
title: "Psychometric Curve Fitting"
output: html_document
---
# Setup
```{r setup, warning=FALSE}
data <- read.csv('Long 98 final.csv')
library(tidyverse)

fit_curve <- function(.data) {
  # This function takes a data frame and fits a logistic curve
  glm(
    accuracy ~ 1 + Range,
    family = binomial("logit"),
    data = .data
  )
}

extract_estimates <- function(model) {
  # This function takes a logistic model and extracts
  # the psychometric curve indices
  coef <- unname(coef(model))
  
  tibble(
    B = 1 / coef[2],
    A = -B * coef[1],
    
    j75 = A - B * log(1 / 0.75 - 1),
    j25 = A - B * log(1 / 0.25 - 1),
    
    JND = (j75 - j25) / 2,
    PSE = A - B * log(1 / 0.50 - 1)
  )
}

add_predictions <- function(model, grid) {
  # This function returns predictions for grid according to the model
  grid$Y_hat <- predict(model, newdata = grid, type = "response")
  grid
}


```
# Fit
```{r data}
# For each subject / task, fit a logistic curve (*add Group*)
data_fits <- data |> 
  group_by(experimentname, subject,Group,regression,Age) |> 
  summarise(
    FIT = list(fit_curve(.data = pick(everything())))
  ) |> 
  ungroup()

# For each subject / task, extract the psychometric curve indices 
data_params <- data_fits |> 
  mutate(
    map(FIT, extract_estimates) |> bind_rows()
  ) |> 
  select(-FIT)
```

Add predictions:

```{r warning=FALSE}
grid <- data.frame(Range = c(0, 15, 21, 27, 33, 39, 45))

# Make predictions
data_predictions <- data_fits |> 
  #add *Group*
  group_by(experimentname,subject,Group,regression,Age) |> 
  reframe(
    add_predictions(FIT[[1]], grid))|> 
  ungroup() |> 
  left_join(data, by = c("experimentname", "subject", "Range","Group","regression","Age"))
# *add Group*
```

# Save results

Add R2

```{r}
data_params2 <- data_predictions |> 
  group_by(experimentname, subject,Group,regression,Age) |> 
  summarise(
    Rsq = cor(accuracy, Y_hat) ^ 2
  ) |> 
  ungroup() |> 
  left_join(data_params, by = join_by(experimentname, subject,Group,regression,Age))
#*add Group*
write.csv(data_predictions, "full_data_with_predictions.csv")
write.csv(data_params2, "full_results.csv")
```

# Plot

```{r}
data_predictions <- read.csv("full_data_with_predictions.csv")

ggplot(data_predictions, aes(X)) +
  facet_grid(rows = vars(Group), 
             cols = vars(experimentname), 
             as.table = FALSE) +
  geom_segment(aes(xend = Range, y = accuracy, yend = Y_hat), alpha = 0.2) +
  geom_line(aes(y = Y_hat, group = subject), alpha = 0.2) +
  geom_point(aes(y = accuracy), alpha = 0.2, shape = 16) +
  stat_summary(aes(y = Y_hat), geom = "line", 
               color = "red", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.7) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1)) +
  theme_bw()
```
```{r}
data_params2 <- read.csv("full_results.csv")

ggplot(data_params2, aes(Rsq)) +
  facet_grid(rows = vars(Group), 
             cols = vars(experimentname), 
             as.table = FALSE) +
  geom_histogram() +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 1)) +
  theme_bw()

ggplot(data_params2, aes(JND)) +
  facet_grid(rows = vars(Group), 
             cols = vars(experimentname), 
             as.table = FALSE) +
  geom_histogram() +
  theme_bw()

ggplot(data_params2, aes(PSE)) +
  facet_grid(rows = vars(Group), 
             cols = vars(experimentname), 
             as.table = FALSE) +
  geom_histogram() +
  theme_bw()
```


```{r}
#data_predictions <- read.csv("full_data_with_predictions.csv")
data_predictions <- child_predictions

ggplot(data_predictions, aes(x = Range)) +
  facet_grid(rows = vars(experimentname),cols = vars(regression), as.table = TRUE) +
  #geom_segment(aes(xend = Range, y = accuracy, yend = Y_hat), alpha = 0.2) +
  geom_line(aes(y = Y_hat, group = subject), alpha = 0.15) +
  geom_point(aes(y = accuracy), alpha = 0.1, shape = 20) +
  stat_summary(aes(y = Y_hat), geom = "line", color = "red", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.5) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1)) +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 50)+
  theme_bw()
  ggsave(filename = "child_plot.png",width = 20,height = 20)

```
```{r}

data_params2 <- read.csv("full_results.csv")

# Rsq histogram
ggplot(data_params2, aes(x = Rsq)) +
  facet_grid(rows = vars(Group), 
             cols = vars(experimentname), 
             as.table = FALSE) +
  geom_histogram() +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 1)) +
  theme_bw()

# JND histogram
ggplot(data_params2, aes(x = JND)) +
  facet_grid(rows = vars(Group), 
             cols = vars(experimentname), 
             as.table = FALSE) +
  geom_histogram() +
  theme_bw()

# PSE histogram
ggplot(data_params2, aes(x = PSE)) +
  facet_grid(rows = vars(Group), 
             cols = vars(experimentname), 
             as.table = FALSE) +
  geom_histogram() +
  theme_bw()

```

# Explantion 

The model we are fitting a 2 parameter mode (A, B):

$$
P = \frac{1}{1 + e^{-(X-A)/B}}
$$
We are doing this by fitting a logistic curve:

$$
P = \frac{1}{1 + e^{-(a + bX)}}
$$
And the converting:

$$
B = 1 / b \\
A = -aB
$$


#PsychoMetric_reg_sep  
```{r}
#this code will plot for biasp&biasm separately 
#psychometric plot for biasp

data_predictions <- read.csv("full_data_with_predictions.csv")

ggplot(data_predictions %>% filter(regression == "biasp"), aes(x = Range, y = Y_hat)) +facet_grid(rows = vars(Group), 
             cols = vars(regression,experimentname), 
             as.table = TRUE) +
  #geom_segment(aes(xend = Range, y = accuracy, yend = Y_hat), alpha = 0.2) +
  geom_line(aes(y = Y_hat, group = subject), alpha = 0.15) +
  geom_point(aes(y = accuracy), alpha = 0.1, shape = 20) +
  stat_summary(aes(y = Y_hat), geom = "line", color = "red", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.5) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1)) +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 40)+
  theme_bw()
  ggsave(filename = "my_plot_biasp.png",width = 20,height = 20)
  
#psychometric plot for biasm

data_predictions <- read.csv("full_data_with_predictions.csv")

ggplot(data_predictions %>% filter(regression == "biasm"), aes(x = Range, y = Y_hat)) +facet_grid(rows = vars(Group), 
             cols = vars(regression,experimentname), 
             as.table = TRUE) +
  #geom_segment(aes(xend = Range, y = accuracy, yend = Y_hat), alpha = 0.2) +
  geom_line(aes(y = Y_hat, group = subject), alpha = 0.15) +
  geom_point(aes(y = accuracy), alpha = 0.1, shape = 20) +
  stat_summary(aes(y = Y_hat), geom = "line", color = "blue", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.5) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, 1)) +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 40)+
  theme_bw()
  ggsave(filename = "my_plot_biasm.png",width = 20,height = 20)

```




#Individual_Psychometric
```{r}
#this chunk wsill plot will make .png file for each subject psychometric function
# Get a list of unique subjects
df <- child_predictions
output_dir <- "subject_child_plots"
unique_subjects <- unique(df$subject)
# Loop through each subject
for (subject in unique_subjects) {
  # Filter data for the current subject 
  subject_data <- df %>% filter(subject == !!subject)
  
  # Create the plot
  plot <- ggplot(subject_data, aes(x = Range)) +
    facet_grid(rows = vars(experimentname), cols = vars(regression), as.table = TRUE) +
    geom_line(aes(y = Y_hat, group = subject), color = "blue", linewidth = 0.7) +
    geom_point(aes(y = accuracy), alpha = 2, shape = 20) +
    #stat_summary(aes(y = Y_hat), geom = "line", color = "blue", linewidth = 0.7, ) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.3) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +
    coord_fixed(ratio = 50) +
    theme_bw() +
    ggtitle(paste("Subject", subject))
  
  # Save the plot to a file
  output_path <- file.path(output_dir, paste0("subject_", subject, ".png"))
  ggsave(filename = output_path, plot = plot, width = 10, height = 10, dpi = 300)
}

cat("All subject plots have been saved in the folder:", output_dir, "\n")

```


#post_data_analysis
```{r}
child_predictions <- data_predictions %>% filter(Group == "child", !(Range %in% c("15", "45")))


```

#group_cor
```{r}
# Filter data for Age > 11
cor_prep <- data_params2 %>% filter(Age > 11)

# Filter for Asian biasm
asian_biasm <- cor_prep %>% filter(exp == "Asian", regression == "biasm")

# Perform correlation test (calculates correlation and p-value)
cor_test <- cor.test(asian_biasm$Age, asian_biasm$JND)

# Print correlation and p-value
cat("Correlation:", cor_test$estimate, "\n")
cat("P-value:", cor_test$p.value, "\n")

# Plot with ggplot2
correlation_plot <- ggplot(asian_biasm, aes(x = Age, y = JND)) +
  geom_point(color = "blue", size = 2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "Correlation between Age and JND (Asian biasm)",
    x = "Age",
    y = "JND",
    caption = paste("Correlation:", round(cor_test$estimate, 2), 
                    "\nP-value:", signif(cor_test$p.value, 3))
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# Print the plot
print(correlation_plot)



```
